# rom.bilinear_form_rom

`rom.bilinear_form_rom`

**Purpose:**  
This module helps turn *full-size* element stiffness matrices into *reduced* (ROM) versions, handling all the index mapping and memory tricks so you get efficient reduced-order stiffness matrices, even for big problems.

**Features:**  
- `BilinearFormROM`: Class for projecting and assembling reduced matrices
- Groups elements by how they hit Dirichlet boundaries (for efficient handling)
- Builds the global reduced stiffness matrix from reduced element contributions—optionally in chunks for better memory use

The `rom` folder also includes:
- Classes for handling reduced assembly and projection (both bilinear and linear forms)
- Helper utilities for Dirichlet BCs in ROMs
- Tricks for chunked and group-wise matrix assembly (so huge problems fit in memory)
- Index/dof mapping tools between full and reduced problems

**[Author: Suparno Bhattacharyya]**

---

## Classes

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [BilinearFormROM](#skrom.rom.bilinear_form_rom.BilinearFormROM) | Main class for assembling reduced-order bilinear forms (ROM stiffness matrices) |

---

### BilinearFormROM

```python
rom.bilinear_form_rom.BilinearFormROM(
    form,
    ubasis,
    lob,
    rob,
    vbasis=None,
    free_dofs=None,
    mean=None,
    nthreads=0,
    dtype=np.float64,
)
```

#### What does this class do?

- Projects element-wise full stiffness matrices onto your reduced bases, then adds them up—for a fast global reduced stiffness matrix
- Handles mapping from “full” degrees of freedom (DOFs) to “free” DOFs (skipping Dirichlet ones)
- Smartly groups elements: some are all-Dirichlet-free, others are mixed—grouping helps make assembly much faster and use less memory
- For huge problems, can assemble in “chunks”—avoiding running out of memory

---

#### Attributes

| Name          | Type                                  | Description                                                       |
|---------------|---------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|
| lob           | (ndarray, shape(N_free, r) or (N, r)) | Left (test) reduced basis                                         |
| rob           | (ndarray, shape(N_free, r) or (N, r)) | Right (trial) reduced basis                                       |
| free_dofs     | ndarray or None                       | Indices of "free" (non-Dirichlet) DOFs                            |
| mean          | ndarray or None                       | Mean field (subtracted from the snapshots before SVD)             |
| nthreads      | int                                   | How many threads to use for parallel computation                  |
| dtype         | data - type                           | Which numeric type (e.g. float64)                                 |
| ubasis        | Basis                                 | Full FE basis (trial functions)                                   |
| vbasis        | Basis                                 | Full FE basis (test functions)                                    |
| mapping       | (ndarray, shape(N_full))              | Maps global DOFs to reduced ones, -1 for Dirichlet ones           |
| element_dofs  | ndarray                               | Local-to-global DOF mapping for each element                      |
| free_indices  | ndarray                               | Which reduced DOF each element maps to                            |
| mask          | ndarray of bool                       | Which DOFs per element are free                                   |
| r             | int                                   | How many reduced basis vectors                                    |
| groupA        | ndarray                               | Indexes of elements with **all free** (easy case)                 |
| groupB        | ndarray                               | Indexes of elements with at least one Dirichlet (mixed case)      |
| chunk_size    | int                                   | How many elements per chunk for groupA assembly                   |
| n_full_chunks | int                                   | Number of full-sized element chunks                               |
| remainder     | int                                   | Number of leftover (not full chunk) elements                      |

---

#### Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [assemble](#skrom.rom.bilinear_form_rom.BilinearFormROM.assemble) | Build the global reduced stiffness matrix from all projected element matrices. |
| [extract_element_matrices](#skrom.rom.bilinear_form_rom.BilinearFormROM.extract_element_matrices) | Get all local full stiffness matrices (per element), ready to be projected.   |

---

##### assemble

```python
rom.bilinear_form_rom.BilinearFormROM.assemble(vbasis=None, **kwargs)
```

**What does it do?**  
Projects every local element stiffness matrix onto the reduced basis, and adds them all up to give the reduced global stiffness matrix, only using “free” DOFs (ignores Dirichlet as needed).

###### Parameters

| Name     | Type   | Description                                              | Default   |
|----------|--------|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------|
| vbasis   | Basis  | FE basis for test functions (default: uses `ubasis`)     | `None`    |
| **kwargs |        | Any extra parameters for the form/assembly process.      | `{}`      |

###### Returns

| Name      | Type                   | Description                        |
|-----------|------------------------|------------------------------------|
| K_reduced | (ndarray, shape(r, r)) | The assembled reduced stiffness matrix |

---

##### extract_element_matrices

```python
rom.bilinear_form_rom.BilinearFormROM.extract_element_matrices(
    ubasis,
    vbasis=None,
    **kwargs,
)
```

**What does it do?**  
For a given (full) bilinear form, returns the stiffness matrix for *each element* in the mesh—a key step before reducing.

###### Parameters

| Name   | Type         | Description                                                          | Default    |
|--------|--------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| form   | BilinearForm | The full-order bilinear form                                         | _required_ |
| ubasis | Basis        | FE basis for the trial function                                      | _required_ |
| vbasis | Basis        | FE basis for the test function (if missing, uses `ubasis`)           | `None`     |
| kwargs | dict         | Extra arguments for assembly                                         | `{}`       |

###### Returns

| Name             | Type    | Description                                                                                                    |
|------------------|---------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| element_matrices | ndarray | Array, shape (n_elements, Nbfun, Nbfun): local stiffness matrix for each element (Nbfun = number of basis funcs)|

---

**In Summary:**
- Use `BilinearFormROM` in your ROM code to efficiently build reduced stiffness matrices, even for huge models with Dirichlet BCs.
- Takes care of all index mapping, boundary condition issues, and can avoid big memory spikes using grouping/chunking.
- Assembly is super-efficient, and works directly with your reduced bases.
